title: The long exhale practice: six rounds
subtitle: A short breath meditation for using the extended exhale to activate the parasympathetic nervous system and move from high-alert into rest.
excerpt: A breath meditation from Day 15 of the SLEEP program. Use to move the body out of sympathetic activation before sleep — six rounds of extended exhale, progressively slower.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 15 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). Extended exhale technique draws on established parasympathetic activation research (Telles et al., 2011; Zaccaro et al., 2018).

practice.whenToUse: Use in the final 30 minutes before sleep, or any time the body feels wound up and needs a fast, evidence-based downshift.

content.text: A five-minute breath practice from Day 15 of the SLEEP program. The extended exhale is one of the most reliable tools for shifting the nervous system from sympathetic activation (alert, ready, tense) into parasympathetic rest. The mechanism is simple: a longer exhale slows the heart rate and signals safety to the brainstem. Six rounds, progressively slower.
Lie down or sit comfortably. Close your eyes. Let your hands rest loose — palms up if you are in bed, palms down on your thighs if sitting.

For each round: inhale through the nose for a count of four. Hold briefly for two counts. Exhale fully through the mouth for a count of six. The exhale should be complete — empty the lungs properly before the next inhale.
Round 1: Inhale 4, hold 2, exhale 6. Notice the body starting to soften.
Round 2: Same count. Drop your shoulders as you exhale.
Round 3: Inhale 4, hold 2, exhale 7. Allow a small, natural pause at the bottom before the next breath.
Round 4: Same count. Let the jaw release. Let the hands go heavy.
Round 5: Inhale 4, hold 2, exhale 8. The exhale is now longer than it feels natural — stay with it.
Round 6: Let the breath return to its own pace. No counting. Just notice how the body has shifted.
After the six rounds, stay still for one or two minutes. The nervous system will continue settling after the last breath.

Adapted from Day 15 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). The extended exhale technique draws on research into vagal activation through breath pattern control (Telles et al., 2011; Zaccaro et al., 2018).
